figures.caption: Figure 1: The PINN solver (top) minimizes the residual loss weighted by a dynamic Gaussian distribution ϕ. Simultaneously, the Residual-based Gaussian Tracking (bottom) performs a linear regression on the PDE residual to center ϕnew in high-error regions. The updated weights are then fed back (red arrow) to focus the PINN’s optimization capacity on the moving discontinuity.
Figure 2: Solutions obtained using G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted formulation 2a, standard PINN 2b and reference solution obtained with Cole-Hopf transformation 2c.
Figure 3: Solutions profiles at different time instants
Figure 4: Absolute error maps of the G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted loss formulation method 4a and the standard PINN 4b.
Figure 5: Solutions obtained using G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted formulation 5a, standard PINN 5b and reference solution obtained with Cole-Hopf transformation 5c.
Figure 6: Solutions profiles at different time instants.
Figure 7: Absolute error maps of the G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted loss formulation method 7a and the standard PINN 7b
Figure 8: Solutions obtained using G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted formulation 8a, standard PINN 8b and reference solution obtained with Cole-Hopf transformation 8c.
Figure 9: Solutions profiles at different time instants.
Figure 10: Absolute error maps of the G-PINNs: Gaussian-based spatially weighted loss formulation method 10a and the standard PINN 10b
Figure 11: Gaussian distributions obtained for the static shock wave and the moving shock wave with ν = 0.0005.
